input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values.

The most common cause: they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel instance.

Another possible cause: the ViewModel properties aren't raising `PropertyChanged` notifications (i.e., not implementing `INotifyPropertyChanged`), but that would only matter if properties are set *after* the binding is established.

Since the labels are completely blank (not just failing to update), the most likely issue is a missing `BindingContext`.


I'll provide a straightforward solution focusing on the most probable culprit.
